free_text: Elastic Scattering of 26 MeV Deuterons
A lithium-drifted semiconductor detector with
an effective area of 1 cm2 was used in the majority of
the measurements. This counter has a sensitive layer of
2.2 mm thickness.
A particle identification system
combined with a 0.3 mm CsI(Tl) crystal, backed by a
4 mm NaI(Tl) crystal was used to remove the background
contribution from the spectra.
In the tilted position of the chamber
normalization was obtained with the help of a NaI(Tl)
attached to the lower part of the scattering chamber.
This counter observed the scattering at 20 degr.
Absolute values of the differential cross-section
were determined by integrating the charge collected in
36 cm long graphite Faraday cup.
Relative errors. The following errors
contributed in the calculations:
(a) The interpretational errors from the uncertainty
in the procedure of extracting peak areas from the
energy spectra;
(b) The statistical errors;
(c) Corrections for finite geometry;
These errors give an uncertainty in the
ordinate scale for an entire angular distribution.
They result of the following factors:
(a) Errors made in the determination of the number of
target atoms.
(b) The charge collection errors are limited to the
integration circuit.
the energy spread of the beam

Natural target of 4 mg/cm2 thick.
The measurements of scattering by Mg at angles
larger than 120 degr. was obtained with the help of the
particle identification system, in conjunction with a
solid state counter telescope. The counter consisted of
a 0.3 mm Au layer surface barrier detector, backed by
a 2 mm Li-drifted detector.
